stem: A decision-maker must choose among 4 energy source options. The probability of favorable regional climate data is uncertain, estimated between 44% and 59%. The payoffs (in $K) for each option are: solar: $219K in good regional climate data, $89K in bad regional climate data. wind: $96K in good regional climate data, $42K in bad regional climate data. natural gas: $229K in good regional climate data, $40K in bad regional climate data. geothermal: $57K in good regional climate data, $18K in bad regional climate data. Which option maximizes expected value? Does the optimal choice change across the range of probability estimates? What is the expected value of the best option?

ground_truth.answer: At P(good)=0.52: best option is solar with EV=$156.6K. solar is optimal across the entire uncertainty range. EV range of best option: [146.2, 165.7].

solution_steps: Compute EV for each option: EV = P(good)×payoff_good + P(bad)×payoff_bad.
solar is optimal across the entire uncertainty range.
common_failure_modes: Evaluating options only at the midpoint probability without sensitivity analysis
Not identifying the breakeven probability where the optimal choice switches
Ignoring downside risk and focusing only on expected value
